home.heroTitle: Welcome to the Official Website of the Holy Family Province
home.heroSubtitle: Inspired by Saint Vincent Pallotti, we are a community of priests and brothers united in the mission of spreading God's love through charity, apostolate, and service.
home.welcomeBadge: A Word from the Provincial Rector
home.rectorQuote: A life dedicated to the service of God and his people in the spirit of St. Vincent Pallotti.
welcomeBody: If for some 'the Internet seems to be a hotbed of vulgar entertainment and superficial content, which thrives on the dark side of our human nature', for us, if we will, it can be an instrument of evangelization and unprecedented fraternal communion.

Even if Vincent Pallotti did not live in the age of the Internet, he was, without a shadow of a doubt, 'a skilful communicator and a man of many relationships' as his epistolary collection testifies. He took advantage of printed materials to disseminate his pastoral and apostolic initiatives. One would dare to say that if our dear Founder were of our time, he would have learned to speak with the language of today and to use the means of communication of our time.

It will be remembered that in the 'Rule' called 'Copia Lambruschini' he suggested: 'all should learn the language of the country where they live, without neglecting culture, the proper language, which will be very useful on the spot, or for the works of the glory of God, and the spiritual advantages of one's neighbor' (OOCC VII, p.162).

This is why I hope that this site will be a way for us to learn the language of the country, of the world in which we live, without neglecting modern or even postmodern culture, but without letting ourselves be chained by its grey areas either.

home.missionQuote: The love of Christ urges us on.

hero.title: Holy Family Province
hero.subtitle: SAC · Kigali, Rwanda
hero.description: Inspired by Saint Vincent Pallotti, we are a community of priests and brothers united in the mission of spreading God's love through charity, apostolate, and service to all people.

founder.title: Saint Vincent Pallotti
founder.subtitle: Founder of the Pallottines
biography: Vincent Pallotti was a descendant of the Pallotti family of Norcia and the De Rossi family of Rome, thus he was of noble ancestry. He completed his early studies at the school of San Pantaleone, then at the college in Rome. At the age of 16, he decided he wanted to become a priest. He was ordained a priest on May 16, 1818, and celebrated his first Mass at the Church of the Gesù (Frascati), at the altar of Our Lady, Refuge of Sinners.

On July 25, he became a doctor of theology and was appointed a professor of theology. He was an excellent theologian who could have had a brilliant career teaching this discipline, but his vocation led him more towards apostolic work.

He traveled throughout the city of Rome, providing material aid and comfort to the poor, preaching the gospel, living on little and sharing what he had, hearing confessions, and spiritually assisting all the faithful who came to him.

Pallotti was convinced of the importance of charity and its practice by all Catholics in order to bring the Good News to everyone. He founded the Society of the Catholic Apostolate (SAC) in 1835, a congregation that continues his mission worldwide to this day.
founder.quote: The love of Christ urges us on.

charisma.title: The Charisma of Saint Vincent Pallotti
paragraphs: Throughout history, God has enriched men and women with the gifts of the Holy Spirit so that the saving mission of Christ might continue for the good of humanity and the building up of the Church.

Saint Vincent Pallotti (1795–1850) belongs to the number of those whom, during the first half of the 19th century, God enriched with his gifts and inspirations, so that they might come to the aid of the Church in the fulfillment of its mission.

Faced with the worsening problems of faith that the Church of his time had to confront, and faced with the multiplication of its tasks for the spread of the Gospel in mission lands, he realized the urgency of reviving faith and rekindling charity among Catholics, with the intention of bringing all people to the unity of faith in Christ.

To achieve this goal, he considered it indispensable to ensure the collaboration of all members of the Church, both clergy and laity, and to unite their efforts in order to promote its apostolic mission more effectively.

history.title: History of the Holy Family Province

chapters.text: It all begins in 1890, when the Holy See asked the German Pallottines to take charge of the territory of Cameroon and appointed Fr. Heinrich Vieter as Apostolic Prefect. He, along with another German Pallottine, Fr. George Walter, and six Pallottine brothers, embarked from Hamburg for Cameroon, arriving on October 25, 1890. Despite the early hardships, they succeeded in laying the foundations of the first Cameroonian mission, which Fr. Heinrich Vieter would call Marienberg (Mount of Mary). On December 8, 1890, they celebrated Mass in the first hut they had just built, and Fr. Heinrich Vieter placed the Cameroon mission under the protection of the Virgin Mary, Queen of the Apostles.

Fr. Alojzy Majewski, who worked as a missionary for four years in Cameroon, left Africa in 1907 and went to Poland to found the very first Pallottine community there, becoming the Founder of the Polish Pallottine Province of Christ the King. It was at the request of Bishop Jean-Baptiste Gahamanyi, the first Bishop of Butare, that the Pallottine Missionaries of the Polish Province of Christ the King arrived in the Great Lakes Region. The first group of 10 missionaries arrived in Rwanda on June 8, 1973. Upon their arrival, they took charge of parishes in the Diocese of Butare at Mugombwa, and later at Kansi and Gikongoro; in the Archdiocese of Kigali at Masaka, Gikondo and Kabuga; and in the Diocese of Kabgayi at Kinazi and Ruhango.

chapters.caption: Mgr Henryk HOSER, one of the first Polish missionaries in Rwanda
chapters.text: In June 1981, the Pallottines began their mission in Zaire (today the Democratic Republic of Congo), serving in the parishes of Rugari and Rutshuru in the Diocese of Goma. In 1986, the first Community was established in Brussels, Belgium. On May 3, 1988, the Delegation became the Holy Family Region, dependent on the Province of Christ the King. On December 27, 2015, this Region was canonically erected as a Province, according to SAC law numbers 89, 311 and 312 and Decision No. 8 of the 20th General Assembly. The Holy Family Province is the first in Africa. It has an international and intercultural character, being composed of 111 members from three countries: Poland, Rwanda and the Democratic Republic of Congo.

communities.title: Communities & Works
communities.subtitle: Serving God's people across Rwanda, DR Congo, Belgium and beyond

items.name: Communauté Sainte Famille de Gikondo
items.description: The Provincial House and Mother House of the Holy Family Province. Seat of provincial government and administration, hosting the Parish of Saint Vincent Pallotti, the Saint Vincent Pallotti Centre, the Caritas Pallottine office, and the Pallottine Foundation for the Family.
items.memberSummary: The Communauté Sainte Famille de Gikondo is named after the Holy Family because it is the seat of the Holy Family Missionary Province, which extends over Rwanda, DR Congo and Belgium. It is often called the 'Mother House' or 'Provincial House'. It serves three main works: the general services of provincial government and administration, the Parish of Saint Vincent Pallotti de Gikondo, and the Saint Vincent Pallotti Centre/Gikondo. It also hosts the Caritas Pallottine office and the Pallottine Foundation for the Family. It is composed of 10 priests, 2 brothers, 1 deacon and 1 seminarian on internship.

items.name: Communauté Immaculée Conception de Kabuga
items.description: Named after the Immaculate Conception, this community serves the Parish of Kabuga and hosts the Saint John Paul II Centre. It is also home to the Shrine of Divine Mercy of Kabuga and supports the Pallottine Foundation for the Family.
items.memberSummary: The Communauté Immaculée Conception de Kabuga serves the Parish of Kabuga in the Archdiocese of Kigali. It hosts the Saint John Paul II Centre and the Shrine of Divine Mercy of Kabuga, and supports the Pallottine Foundation for the Family (FPF).

items.name: Community of Ruhango
items.description: Serving the Diocese of Kabgayi, this community runs the Parish of Ruhango and hosts the Shrine of Jesus the Merciful, which includes a chapel of perpetual adoration and healing prayer gatherings.
